era_context: Second Intermediate Period in Egypt; Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia under Samsu-iluna; mature Indus Valley Civilization; Hittite Old Kingdom; early Shang dynasty in China (traditional dating).

events.title: Samsu-iluna's reign in Babylon
events.description: Samsu-iluna continued his rule over Babylon. Cuneiform records document the administrative functioning of the Old Babylonian state. The reign represents a period of relative stability, though the dynasty's long-term decline was underway.
events.certainty_note: Attested in cuneiform sources; absolute dating uncertain due to chronological debates.

historiographic_note: This year is not distinguished by any specific documented event. The five-year span from -1839 to -1835 is characterized by administrative continuity under Samsu-iluna in Babylon and the ongoing mature phase of the Indus Valley Civilization. The historical record for this period is thin, consisting primarily of administrative documents that do not provide year-by-year narrative detail. The chronological framework itself remains contested among scholars.
